mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment is already above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the strategy falls below 0.5 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which provides immediate positive earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day returns in >55% of expansion months, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of high-inflation months, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes when core inflation remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following above-trend GDP prints, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, prompting risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production contracts, or positive returns dominate (>55% of cases) during late-cycle phases.
